name: George Carlin
abstract: George Denis Patrick Carlin (May 12, 1937 – June 22, 2008) was an American stand-up comedian, social critic, actor and author. He was known for his dark comedy and reflections on politics, English, psychology, religion, and taboo subjects.

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Barbutti
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t think he’d been on stage for five minutes when he’d use most of the profanity that George Carlin had written about.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Barbutti interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 8, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: I liked George Carlin after that, I had all — three or four — three of his records.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 18, 2023 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: Well I always liked George Carlin and just the real good comics I loved to see
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999
